global.description: Configures global settings which impact all targets.
global.title: Global

file_watch_config.description: Configure how frequently log files from disk get polled for changes.
file_watch_config.title: File Watch Config

disable.description: Disable the HTTP and GRPC server.
disable.title: Disable

profiling_enabled.description: Enable the /debug/fgprof and /debug/pprof endpoints for profiling.
profiling_enabled.title: Profiling Enable

http_listen_port.description: HTTP server listen port (0 means random port).
http_listen_port.title: Http Listen Port

grpc_listen_port.description: gRPC server listen port (0 means random port).
grpc_listen_port.title: Grpc Listen Port

grpc_server_max_recv_msg_size.description: Max gRPC message size that can be received.
grpc_server_max_recv_msg_size.title: Grpc Server Max Recv Msg Size

grpc_server_max_send_msg_size.description: Max gRPC message size that can be sent.
grpc_server_max_send_msg_size.title: Grpc Server Max Send Msg Size

grpc_server_max_concurrent_streams.description: Limit on the number of concurrent streams for gRPC calls (0 = unlimited).
grpc_server_max_concurrent_streams.title: Grpc Server Max Concurrent Streams

log_level.description: Log only messages with the given severity or above. Supported values [debug, info, warn, error].
log_level.title: Log Level

http_path_prefix.description: Base path to server all API routes from (e.g., /v1/).
http_path_prefix.title: Http Path Prefix

health_check_target.description: Target managers check flag for Promtail readiness, if set to false the check is ignored.
health_check_target.title: Health Check Target

clients.description: Describes how Promtail connects to multiple instances of Grafana Loki, sending logs to each.
clients.title: Clients

url.description: The URL where Loki is listening, denoted in Loki as http_listen_address and http_listen_port.
url.title: URL

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers to be sent along with each push request.
headers.title: HTTP headers

tenant_id.description: The tenant ID used by default to push logs to Loki.
tenant_id.title: Tenant Id

batchwait.description: Maximum amount of time to wait before sending a batch, even if that batch isn't full.
batchwait.title: Batchwait

batchsize.description: Maximum batch size (in bytes) of logs to accumulate before sending the batch to Loki.
batchsize.title: Batchsize

basic_auth.description: If using basic auth, configures the username and password sent.
basic_auth.title: Basic Auth

username.description: The username to use for basic auth.
username.title: Username

password.description: The password to use for basic auth.
password.title: Password

password_file.description: The file containing the password for basic auth.
password_file.title: Password File

bearer_token.description: Bearer token to send to the server.
bearer_token.title: Bearer Token

bearer_token_file.description: File containing bearer token to send to the server.
bearer_token_file.title: Bearer Token File

proxy_url.description: HTTP proxy server to use to connect to the server.
proxy_url.title: Proxy URL

drop_rate_limited_batches.description: Disable retries of batches that Loki responds to with a 429 status code (TooManyRequests).
drop_rate_limited_batches.title: Proxy URL

timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a server to respond to a request.
timeout.title: Timeout

positions.description: Describes how to save read file offsets to disk.
positions.title: Positions

sync_period.description: How often to update the positions file.
sync_period.title: Sync Period

ignore_invalid_yaml.description: Whether to ignore & later overwrite positions files that are corrupted.
ignore_invalid_yaml.title: Ignore Invalid Yaml

scrape_configs.description: Configures how Promtail can scrape logs from a series of targets.
scrape_configs.title: Scrape Configs

scrape_config.description: Configures how Promtail can scrape logs from a series of targets.
scrape_config.title: Scrape Configs

job_name.description: Name to identify this scrape config in the Promtail UI.
job_name.title: Job Name

decompression.description: Defines decompression behavior for the given scrape target.
decompression.title: decompression

enabled.description: Whether decompression should be tried or not.
enabled.title: Enabled

initial_delay.description: Initial delay to wait before starting the decompression.
initial_delay.title: Initial Delay

targets.title: Targets
targets.description: Configures the discovery to look on the current machine. This is required by the prometheus service discovery code but doesn't really apply to Promtail which can ONLY look at files on the local machine. As such it should only have the value of localhost, OR it can be excluded entirely and a default value of localhost will be applied by Promtail.

labels.description: Defines a file to scrape and an optional set of additional labels to apply to all streams defined by the files from __path__.
labels.title: Lables

__path__.description: The path to load logs from. Can use glob patterns (e.g., /var/log/*.log).
__path__.title: Path

__path_exclude__.description: Used to exclude files from being loaded. Can also use glob patterns.
__path_exclude__.title: Path Exclude

[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*.description: Additional labels to assign to the logs
[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*.title: Additional Label

readline_rate_enabled.description: When true, enforces rate limiting on this instance of Promtail.
readline_rate_enabled.title: Readline Rate Enable

readline_rate.description: The rate limit in log lines per second that this instance of Promtail may push to Loki.
readline_rate.title: Readline Rate

readline_burst.description: The cap in the quantity of burst lines that this instance of Promtail may push to Loki.
readline_burst.title: Readline Burst

readline_rate_drop.description: When true, exceeding the rate limit causes this instance of Promtail to discard log lines, rather than sending them to Loki. When false, exceeding the rate limit causes this instance of Promtail to temporarily hold off on sending the log lines and retry later.
readline_rate_drop.title: Readline Rate Drop

max_streams.description: Limits the max number of active streams.
max_streams.title: Max Streams

max_line_size.description: Maximum log line byte size allowed without dropping. Example: 256kb, 2M. 0 to disable.
max_line_size.title: Max Line Size

max_line_size_truncate.description: Whether to truncate lines that exceed max_line_size. No effect if max_line_size is disabled.
max_line_size_truncate.title: Max Line Size Truncate

target_config.description: Configures how tailed targets will be watched.
target_config.title: Target Config

sync_period.description: Period to resync directories being watched and files being tailed to discover new ones or stop watching removed ones.
sync_period.title: Sync Period

options_config.$comment: Missing details on the documentation, might be called simply 'option'
options_config.title: Options Config

pipeline_stages.description: Describes how to transform logs from targets.
pipeline_stages.title: Pipeline Stages

pipeline_stage_docker.description: Parses the contents of logs from Docker containers.
pipeline_stage_docker.title: Docker

pipeline_stage_cri.description: Parses the contents of logs from CRI containers.
pipeline_stage_cri.title: Cri

pipeline_stage_regex.description: takes a regular expression and extracts captured named groups to be used in further stages.
pipeline_stage_regex.title: Regex

expression.description: The RE2 regular expression. Each capture group must be named.
expression.title: Expression

source.description: Name from extracted data to parse. If empty, uses the log message.
source.title: Source

pipeline_stage_json.description: Parses a log line as JSON and takes JMESPath expressions to extract data from the JSON to be used in further stages.
pipeline_stage_json.title: Json

expressions.description: Set of key/value pairs of JMESPath expressions. The key will be the key in the extracted data while the expression will be the value, evaluated as a JMESPath from the source data.
expressions.title: Expressions

source.description: Name from extracted data to parse. If empty, uses the log message.
source.title: Source

pipeline_stage_template.description: Uses Go's text/template language to manipulate values.
pipeline_stage_template.title: Template

source.description: Name from extracted data to parse. If key in extract data doesn't exist, an entry for it will be created.
source.title: Source

template.description: Go template string to use. In additional to normal template functions, ToLower, ToUpper, Replace, Trim, TrimLeft, TrimRight, TrimPrefix, TrimSuffix, and TrimSpace are available as functions.
template.title: Template

pipeline_stage_match.description: Conditionally executes a set of stages when a log entry matches a configurable LogQL stream selector.
pipeline_stage_match.title: Match

stages.description: Nested set of pipeline stages only if the selector matches the labels of the log entries.
stages.title: Stages

source.description: Name from extracted data to use for the timestamp.
source.title: Source

format.description: Determines how to parse the time string. Can use pre-defined formats by name: [ANSIC UnixDate RubyDate RFC822 RFC822Z RFC850 RFC1123 RFC1123Z RFC3339 RFC3339Nano Unix UnixMs UnixUs UnixNs].
format.title: Format

source.description: Name from extracted data to use for the log entry.
source.title: Source

pipeline_stage_metrics.description: A map where the key is the name of the metric and the value is a specific metric type.
pipeline_stage_metrics.title: Metrics
